title: Aurelius Autonomous Implementation Loop v7

current_state_anchor.note: Anchor only. Loop must re-derive state from filesystem each cycle (loop_protocol step 1).

identity.summary: Aurelius is a frontier-tier agentic coding LLM platform, a model family, an original terminal/IDE UI surface, and a production-deployable serving stack. Target parity tier: Claude 4.x, GPT-5.x, Gemini 2.5, Llama 4, Qwen3, DeepSeek V3.x, Kimi K2.x, GLM-5, GPT-OSS.
identity.branding_rule: UI may be inspired by terminal coding-agent workflows (Claude Code, kimi-cli, openclaw, Aider, Cline, Continue, Goose), but all branding, mascot art, copy, animations, and visible string assets must be original to Aurelius. No copying of attribution banners, ASCII art, mascot names, or trademarked phrases.

frontier_parity_targets.rationale: Each named target binds harvesting cycles to concrete sources. The loop must, on average, touch one bullet per cycle; cycles that touch zero must justify in the journal.

agent_harness_targets.rationale: These are not models — they are agent harnesses. Harvest loop structure, tool-call formats, plan/act/observe/reflect topology, and eval scaffolding only. Do not copy branding or system prompts verbatim.

runtime.adapter_rule: Foreign-framework code must be isolated behind Aurelius-native interfaces under src/backends/. Core src/model/, src/inference/, src/training/ paths must not import vLLM/SGLang/llama.cpp/JAX/MLX/TRT directly.

core_dep_policy.definition: 'Core' means the inference + agent + UI runtime path. Research/training/data deps may live in [project.dependencies] for developer convenience, but inference/serving/agent/ui code paths must remain importable without them.
core_dep_policy.isolation_rule: Modules under src/inference/, src/serving/, src/agent/, src/ui/, src/cli/, src/model/ (excluding optional research wrappers), and src/backends/ (excluding the explicitly-named foreign adapter file) MUST import from {torch, tokenizers, safetensors, einops, numpy, pyyaml, rich, stdlib, project-local} only. transformers/trl/peft/accelerate are permitted in src/training/, src/alignment/, src/data/loaders/, src/eval/ (harness wrappers), and src/backends/transformers_adapter.py only.
core_dep_policy.enforcement: loop_protocol step 'Foreign-import check' enforces the isolation_rule above; violation is a fail.

rules: AureliusConfig is the backbone runtime config for one variant, not the dumping ground for chat/agent/safety/serving/backend/UI/multimodal behavior.
Prefer family manifests, variant configs, adapters, or downstream surface configs over adding booleans to the backbone config.

Any change to model identity, checkpoint format, tokenizer contract, backend contract, modality contract, tool-call schema, or release routing must include compatibility tests and a manifest/version bump.
Every released variant must ship a model card AND an eval card AND a dataset card before promotion to a stable release track.

reference_backbones.rationale: A family of backbones, not one. Each is a starting point for variants. Backbone selection is a manifest decision, not a global config flag.
reference_backbones.forward_contract: Public behavior returns (loss, logits, cache) or a backend-neutral equivalent through Aurelius wrappers. Multimodal variants additionally accept (image_inputs, audio_inputs) keyword args and route through the modality contract.

prime_directive: Never stop unless genuinely out of ideas across ALL surfaces and ALL frontier_parity_targets. If a direction feels exhausted, pivot to family scaffolding, backend routing, UI/UX, manifest/schema work, variant routing, multimodal modules, computer-use tooling, MCP/tooling ecosystem, production-deployment artifacts, or the next surface area. Do not ask whether to continue. Fatigue, repeated failures, long cycle time, and user silence are not stop conditions. 'Production-ready' means deployable AND tested AND documented AND eval-carded — not just unit-green.

implementation_scope.rule: Every cycle produces wired-in code, not orphan files.
extra_requirements: If the work touches model identity, checkpointing, tokenizer contracts, backend contracts, modality contracts, tool-call schemas, or release routing, also produce or update the family manifest and compatibility tests.
If the work touches a public API surface (CLI, serving, agent harness), update docs/<surface>.md or README.md surface table.
Every 10 cycles, at least one cycle must produce a deployable artifact: container image build, helm chart, served-endpoint smoke, or model card + eval card pair.

A module is not done until it can be constructed from a family manifest or AureliusConfig, invoked through the relevant surface, the full suite stays green, and (if user-facing) it is reflected in README.md.

loop_protocol: Inventory — ls src/*/ tests/*/. Filesystem is the source of truth.
Baseline green — .venv/bin/python3.14 -m pytest --tb=no -q before dispatch. If red, fix first.

Pick 4-6 independent modules from Priority Areas or Idea Reservoir, spanning at least 2 surface areas. If the family contract is incomplete, include at least one family-infrastructure or backend-adapter module.
Dispatch all agents in parallel in one message with multiple tool calls.
Verify unit suite — .venv/bin/python3.14 -m pytest --tb=no -q. Must be green before integration.
On red: git restore offending module+test+integration edits; re-dispatch with failure log. Never --no-verify. Never commit red.

Foreign-import check: core modules must not import research/runtime frameworks directly. Allowed exceptions are src/backends/, src/ui/, src/data/loaders/, tests/backends/, tests/ui/, and explicit export/conversion scripts. Any foreign import in core modules is a fail.
Integration phase — wire each merged module at seams. Additive-within-file only. Never touch Frozen Files.
Commit — git add -A && git commit -m "feat(cycle-N): ... (+integration)" matching existing cycle-numbering.

Push every 3 cycles, or immediately if the cycle touched >10 files. On every push: update README.md surface table + cycle/test counters, ensure LICENSE (MIT, Christien Antonio 2026), and run `gh repo edit S3nna13/Aurelius --description "..."` to refresh About.
Every 5 cycles: git diff HEAD~5 -- <frozen files> — any change is a red flag; investigate.
Every 10 cycles: one cycle must produce a deployable artifact (container, helm, served endpoint smoke, or model+eval card pair).
Journal — .aurelius-cycles.log: cycle-N | modules | surfaces | duration | integrated=[...] | deferred=[...] | sources=[...]
Go to step 1. Never pause for confirmation.

agent_prompt_template.summary: Implement [MODULE_NAME] for Aurelius at ~/Desktop/Aurelius. Production-grade, not research toy.
deliverables: If user-facing: README.md surface row + docs/<surface>.md update
integration_rules: Use additive edits only in existing files.
If model identity, tokenizer, checkpointing, backend selection, modality contract, tool-call schema, or release routing changes, also ship the manifest update and compatibility tests.
Cite source in module docstring: 'Inspired by <repo>@<commit-or-tag>, license <SPDX>, clean-room reimplementation.'

unit_test_rigor_floor: Shape/dtype correctness on tiny config where applicable
Determinism under torch.manual_seed or backend-equivalent seed control
Edge cases: batch=1, seq_len=1, empty input, masked/padded inputs
No NaN/Inf; parsers/dispatchers/safety/UI state machines must not crash on malformed input
Equivalence to reference formulation where one exists (atol=1e-5)
For agent/chat/safety modules: adversarial inputs, malformed tool calls, role confusion
For long-context modules: correctness at seq_len=1, 512, 8192, and extrapolated length when applicable
For UI modules: keyboard navigation, reduced-motion behavior, empty/welcome states, task/status transitions, no crash on missing assets
For multimodal modules: mismatched modality counts, zero-image input, oversized image batches, truncated audio
For computer-use modules: malformed accessibility trees, missing target elements, denied permissions
For MCP modules: malformed tool manifests, server timeouts, schema-version mismatch
For deployment artifacts: container builds reproducibly, healthcheck returns 200, graceful SIGTERM
production_readiness_floor: Structured JSON logs at INFO with trace/span IDs
OpenTelemetry traces on the request path (serving + agent loop)
Prometheus-shaped /metrics endpoint for serving and training
Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM with in-flight request drain
Reproducible container image (Dockerfile + lockfile + SBOM via syft or equivalent)
Model card per released variant (model_card.md), eval card (eval_card.md), dataset card (dataset_card.md)
License-provenance manifest aggregating SPDX of every harvested source
Latency p50/p95/p99 + tokens/sec measured and recorded in eval card
Secrets never logged; PII/refusal classifiers wired into serving middleware
CI green: unit + integration + lint + type-check + container-build + smoke

core: Backend-agnostic runtime; PyTorch is the default backend for current core modules, not the only option.
Canonical core dependencies must stay minimal (see runtime.core_dep_policy).
Optional backend/UI/research extras may be added only when isolated behind adapters or [project.optional-dependencies] groups.
Tiny test config: n_layers=2, d_model=64, n_heads=4, n_kv_heads=2, head_dim=16, d_ff=128, vocab_size=256, max_seq_len=64.
New config keys default to feature OFF.
For agent/safety/UI/computer-use/MCP modules: treat all inputs as untrusted.
Prefer family manifests and adapters over more booleans in AureliusConfig.
Preserve provenance and license terms in manifests, model cards, dataset cards, and release notes.
Do not add a new backbone boolean when the right abstraction is a family manifest, variant config, backend adapter, modality projector, or UI surface config.

Clean-room only when license is restrictive (GPL, AGPL, custom non-commercial). Permissive (MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD) sources may be adapted with attribution in the module docstring + LICENSES_THIRD_PARTY.md.

model_family_training: Family manifests, variant registries, compatibility validation, tokenizer/version contracts, checkpoint migration
Scaling laws, adapters, heads, routing, distillation, model soups, merging, warm-starting, continual pretraining
agent_tool_use: Harness parity studies vs SWE-agent, OpenDevin, Aider, Cline, Continue, Goose, openclaw, ironclaw, Kimi-Dev
chat_instruction_following: Chat template registry (ChatML, Llama3, Harmony, Qwen, DeepSeek)
computer_use_browser_use: GUI action prediction (click, type, scroll, drag)

workflow: Research official Hugging Face model cards, GitHub repos, papers, and dataset cards.
Build a provenance matrix before implementation: source, license, allowed use, backend, compatibility, datasets, migration notes.
Harvest algorithms and data recipes, not branding or hidden notices.
For UI/UX references, borrow layout, state flow, and motion timing only; build original Aurelius assets and animation language.
If the source is permissive (MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD), adapt it into Aurelius-native modules with attribution in module docstring + LICENSES_THIRD_PARTY.md.
If the source is restrictive (GPL/AGPL/custom non-commercial), reimplement clean-room and keep it reference-only; do NOT vendor.
If source code is written against another backend, implement a backend adapter or clean-room translation instead of forcing PyTorch-only.
Preserve provenance and license terms in manifests, model cards, dataset cards, and release notes.
Use external hubs and tooling only in isolated research/export/conversion steps; do not leak those dependencies into the canonical core runtime.

sources.verification_protocol: Before citing a pinned repo in a cycle journal, the harvesting agent MUST `gh repo view <org/name>` (or curl the API) to confirm the repo is non-empty and matches the expected scope. HTTP 200 alone is not proof of content. If verification fails, swap to the documented fallback or open .aurelius-blacklist.log with the reason.
verified_live_2026_04_23: All 32 pinned repos returned HTTP 200 except the original onnx/onnxruntime entry (corrected to microsoft/onnxruntime). openclaw/clawhub, openclaw/openclaw, nearai/ironclaw responded 200 but content scope unverified — the loop's first cycle that targets one of these MUST run `gh repo view` to confirm it is the expected agent-shell repo before harvesting; if scope is wrong, fall back to MoonshotAI/Kimi-Dev or nearai/ironclaw and log in .aurelius-blacklist.log.

hard_rules: No foreign imports in canonical core modules
Widen the net before giving up
Every cycle must cite at least one source repo or paper in its journal entry

frozen_files.rationale: Frozen Files are stable contract surfaces that have not changed in 15+ days and underpin compatibility for downstream variants/adapters. Additive edits OK only via clearly-marked extension points (registries, factories). Schema/signature changes require a manifest version bump and compatibility tests.
explicitly_NOT_frozen: src/model/config.py — actively evolving, additive AureliusConfig fields permitted
src/model/__init__.py — registry root, additive registration permitted
src/__init__.py — top-level export surface, additive permitted
frozen_files.review_cadence: Every 5 cycles, re-evaluate the list: any file in 'files' that received a non-additive change is a hard stop; any file in 'explicitly_NOT_frozen' that has been quiet for 15+ days is a candidate to promote to frozen.

anti_stagnation_rules: 2 consecutive red cycles → reduce cycle size to 2 modules, seminal papers only
Foreign-import grep catches 3 cycles running → cycle-level reminder to grep before reporting done
Frozen-file change → hard stop, revert, re-dispatch with explicit list
Integration repeatedly breaks suite → dedicate next cycle to orphan integration + regression tests
Single-surface cycles 3 in a row → force next cycle to span ≥ 3 surfaces
UI surface < 25 modules after 5 more cycles → force next cycle to be UI-only (terminal shell, command palette, transcript viewer, panels, motion, onboarding, status hierarchy)
Backends surface < 4 distinct adapter implementations after 5 more cycles → force next cycle to add vLLM or SGLang or llama.cpp or MLX adapter
Multimodal surface < 8 modules after 3 more cycles → force next cycle to be multimodal-only
Computer-use surface < 6 modules after 5 more cycles → force computer-use cycle (screen parser, GUI action, browser driver, action verifier)
MCP surface absent after next 3 cycles → force MCP server + client + manifest validator
10 cycles since last deployment artifact → force production-deployment cycle (container, healthz, /metrics, model card, eval card)
Core dep contract violated (research framework imported outside src/backends|data/loaders|ui) → force remediation cycle moving deps to optional extras
"Not sure what to build" → Reservoir → orphans → decomposition → check surface coverage tracker for under-served areas → check frontier_parity_targets for under-cited targets

Out-of-ideas requires evidence that Priority Areas AND Reservoir are exhausted, all orphans integrated, existing modules fully decomposed, every surface has ≥ 25 modules, every frontier_parity_targets entry has been cited in ≥ 3 cycles, and a deployable artifact exists for the latest variant.
